title: MCP Trust Registry — Manifest
description: Schema for an MCP server manifest as defined in protocol v0.1 §4. A manifest is the canonical declaration of a specific server version's identity, capabilities, and signing metadata. Canonicalization rules for hashing live in §4.4.

schema.description: Schema identifier. Implementations MUST refuse manifests with any other value.

name.description: Lowercase, dash-separated server identifier. Globally unique within a registry namespace.

oidc_subject.description: If keyless, the OIDC subject URI (e.g., a GitHub identity).

oidc_issuer.description: If keyless, the OIDC issuer URI (e.g., GitHub's actions OIDC issuer).

type.description: Artifact packaging convention; controls how sha256 is computed (see §4.5).

version.description: For type=npm, the package version (may differ from the manifest version).

fetch_url.description: Origin URL from which the artifact can be fetched.

transport.description: Subset of MCP-defined transports (stdio, http, etc.).

required.description: Capabilities the server cannot function without. Verifier MUST refuse if any are denied at install time.

optional.description: Capabilities the server uses if granted but can operate without. Default to deny under strict policy.

metadata.description: Informational. Verifiers MUST NOT use these fields for trust decisions.

ed25519_key_id.description: ed25519 public key encoded as 'ed25519:' + lowercase base32 (no padding) of the 32-byte key.

sha256_hex.description: Lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest, no prefix.

capability_token.description: Capability token of form 'category:action[:scope]' per §5. Examples: 'fs:read:/tmp/**', 'net:fetch:api.github.com', 'shell:exec', 'secret:read:GITHUB_TOKEN', 'mcp:call:other.tool'. Future categories (gpu:*, usb:*) are accepted syntactically; verifier behaviour for unknown categories is policy-dependent.
